summary: A superb 1990 soundboard capture featuring a revitalized first set (Ramble on Rose, It's All Over Now) and a soaring Dark Star suite in the second, all delivered with crystal‑clear mix quality.
review: The Miller board from September 20 1990 is praised as one of the best‑recorded shows of the ’90s – crisp, well‑balanced and even offered in a seamless Tetzeli redo. Musically the concert shines, with a high‑energy first set highlighted by Ramble on Rose and an incredible It’s All Over Now, followed by a landmark Dark Star suite and tight second‑set work like Truckin' into China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are highly favorable (4–5 stars), emphasizing both excellent soundboard quality and an outstanding performance that ranks among the best of 1990.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp mix; considered the best board for this show. Original release has a slight disc‑to‑disc transition issue that is fixed in the Bill Tetzeli redo.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds strong momentum with revitalized numbers and a rousing crowd, while the second set sustains the high energy with a top‑grade Dark Star suite and seamless transitions, earning an overall A rating.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Muffs verses on Althea but delivers powerful fills on Truckin' and drives the epic Dark Star; overall praised for his lead work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Very active on the upper board, laying down energetic bass lines throughout the second set and opening Touch of Grey with authority.
band_member_comments.Bob: Excited vocal delivery on Greatest Story Ever Told, adding to the show’s enthusiasm.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards are mixed high yet played with a lighter touch; his performance shines across both sets.
